meta.name: MAIM ABC Database
meta.sourceRule: Use this file as the Phase 2 data source for ABC lessons, downloads, videos, prompts, landing-page sections, and future Airtable sync.

records.title: Awareness
records.lesson: Notice the opportunity before you chase the tool. AI starts when you can see where time, energy, attention, or money is leaking.
records.exercise: Write down three tasks you repeat every week. Circle the one that feels most draining.
records.prompt: Act as my AI workflow coach. Help me identify three repeating tasks in my week that could become a simple AI-assisted workflow. Ask me one question at a time.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Where is your time leaking?
records.cta: Comment AWARE when you find one task AI can help with.

records.title: Belief
records.lesson: Belief comes before skill. If you can explain what you need in plain language, you can begin using AI.
records.exercise: Ask AI one question about a real problem in your life, work, family, or business.
records.prompt: You are a beginner-friendly AI guide. Explain how someone like me can start using AI today without technical language. Make it practical and encouraging.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: If you can text, you can use AI.
records.cta: Comment BELIEVE if you are done waiting for perfect.

records.title: Context
records.lesson: AI does not read your mind. Better context creates better output because the tool can only work with what you give it.
records.exercise: Rewrite one vague prompt with your goal, audience, situation, and desired format.
records.prompt: Improve this prompt by adding context, goal, audience, constraints, and output format: [paste my rough prompt]. Then explain why the new version is stronger.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Clear input, clear output.
records.cta: Comment CONTEXT if your second prompt got better.

records.title: Direction
records.lesson: A powerful tool still needs a destination. Direction turns AI from noise into leverage.
records.exercise: Finish this sentence: I want AI to help me accomplish...
records.prompt: Help me turn this goal into a clear AI task: [insert goal]. Give me the outcome, first step, best tool type, and a simple prompt to begin.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Aim AI at your goal.
records.cta: Comment DIRECTION with the outcome you want this week.

records.title: Experiment
records.lesson: Experiment means testing one small idea before turning it into a system. AI gets useful when you try, observe, adjust, and try again.
records.exercise: Run one small AI test today: rewrite a message, summarize a note, draft a caption, map a task, or compare two options.
records.prompt: Help me design a small AI experiment I can run today. My idea or problem is: [insert idea]. Give me the test, the expected result, how to measure it, and what to change if it does not work.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Run the small test before the big build.
records.cta: Comment EXPERIMENT after you run one small AI test.

records.title: Flow
records.lesson: AI becomes powerful when it fits into a repeatable flow instead of staying as a one-off prompt.
records.exercise: Map one workflow you repeat every week from start to finish.
records.prompt: Turn this repeated workflow into an AI-assisted flow: [describe workflow]. Identify each step, where AI can help, and what human judgment should stay with me.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: From prompt to workflow.
records.cta: Comment FLOW if you mapped one repeatable process.

records.title: Growth
records.lesson: AI confidence grows through practice, feedback, and adjustment. You get better by using it, not by watching from the sidelines.
records.exercise: Run three versions of the same prompt and compare what changed.
records.prompt: Give me three versions of this prompt: beginner, sharper, and expert. Then explain what improved in each version: [paste prompt].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Practice beats perfection.
records.cta: Comment GROWTH if you practiced instead of overthinking.

records.title: Human
records.lesson: The tool can generate, but the human still gives taste, values, lived experience, and trust.
records.exercise: Take one AI answer and edit it until it sounds like you.
records.prompt: Rewrite this AI draft so it keeps my meaning but sounds more human, warm, direct, and like my voice: [paste draft]. Ask me for voice examples if needed.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Keep the human in the loop.
records.cta: Comment HUMAN if you rewrote the output in your voice.

records.title: Imagination
records.lesson: AI helps you see options faster. It can turn rough thoughts into drafts, directions, scenes, and prototypes.
records.exercise: Ask AI to turn one dream idea into three possible first versions.
records.prompt: Take this rough idea and imagine three practical versions I could build or test this month: [insert idea]. Include the easiest first version.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Turn the idea into a first draft.
records.cta: Comment IMAGINE if you want to see your idea take shape.

records.title: Judgment
records.lesson: AI can sound confident and still be wrong. Judgment is the human layer that checks truth, fit, tone, and consequence.
records.exercise: Review one AI answer and mark what you would verify, edit, or reject.
records.prompt: Review this AI output with human judgment. Flag weak claims, missing context, tone issues, risks, and what I should verify before using it: [paste output].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Verify before you trust.
records.cta: Comment JUDGMENT if you checked before sharing.

records.title: Knowledge
records.lesson: Knowledge becomes real when you can explain it, apply it, and return to it later.
records.exercise: Teach one AI tip you learned today to another person in plain language.
records.prompt: Turn this AI concept into a simple lesson I can explain to a beginner in under two minutes: [insert concept]. Include an example and one action.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: If you can teach it, you own it.
records.cta: Comment KNOWLEDGE with the tip you can now teach.

records.title: Leverage
records.lesson: Leverage means one good input can create repeated value. AI helps when it turns effort into reusable assets.
records.exercise: Create one reusable template for a task you do every week.
records.prompt: Help me create a reusable template for this weekly task: [describe task]. Make it simple, repeatable, and easy to customize.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Build once, reuse often.
records.cta: Comment LEVERAGE if you created something reusable.

records.title: Mindset
records.lesson: Tools change fast, but mindset travels. Curiosity, responsibility, and action help you adapt as AI evolves.
records.exercise: Finish this sentence: My AI mindset is...
records.prompt: Help me define my AI mindset in one sentence based on my goals, fears, strengths, and current stage: [describe yourself].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Mindset outlasts the tool.
records.cta: Comment MINDSET with one word for your approach.

records.title: Network
records.lesson: Learning gets easier when ideas move through people. Community makes examples, questions, and wins visible.
records.exercise: Share one AI lesson with one person or one group today.
records.prompt: Help me write a short message sharing one AI lesson with my community in a helpful, non-hype way: [insert lesson].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Do not learn alone.
records.cta: Tag someone who should start this roadmap with you.

records.title: Opportunity
records.lesson: Opportunity often starts as a small experiment before it looks like a big win.
records.exercise: Use AI to improve one thing you already do for work, content, family, school, or business.
records.prompt: Find three practical AI opportunities inside this work I already do: [describe work]. Rank them by easiest, most useful, and highest upside.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Start with a tiny test.
records.cta: Comment OPPORTUNITY with what you improved.

records.title: Prompt
records.lesson: A prompt is an instruction. Better prompts give role, goal, context, constraints, and format.
records.exercise: Use the frame: Act as [role]. Help me [goal] for [audience] in [format].
records.prompt: Act as my prompt coach. Improve this prompt using role, goal, context, constraints, examples, and output format: [paste prompt].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Better prompts make AI feel smarter.
records.cta: Comment PROMPT if you want the beginner prompt pack.

records.title: Questions
records.lesson: The next useful question is often where the clarity starts. Follow-ups turn okay answers into better ones.
records.exercise: Ask one follow-up: Can you simplify that? Can you give three options? What am I missing?
records.prompt: Ask me five follow-up questions that would help you give a better answer to this problem: [describe problem]. Wait for my answers before solving.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Ask the next better question.
records.cta: Comment QUESTIONS with your best follow-up prompt.

records.title: Responsibility
records.lesson: Powerful tools require responsible habits. Check facts, protect sensitive information, and respect people.
records.exercise: Review one AI output for errors, bias, private details, or anything that should not be shared.
records.prompt: Audit this AI output for accuracy, privacy, bias, tone, and risk. Tell me what to fix before I use it: [paste output].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Fast is not enough.
records.cta: Comment RESPONSIBLE if you fixed something before posting.

records.title: Skills
records.lesson: Skills turn AI from entertainment into capability. Summarizing, rewriting, planning, researching, and organizing improve with reps.
records.exercise: Practice one AI skill three times today: summarize, brainstorm, rewrite, plan, or organize.
records.prompt: Help me practice the AI skill of [skill]. Give me three short exercises, review my attempts, and tell me what improved.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Build skills, not opinions.
records.cta: Comment SKILLS with the skill you are building.

records.title: Time
records.lesson: AI should give time back for higher-value work, deeper thought, better service, and rest.
records.exercise: Use AI on one repetitive task and spend the saved minutes on something more valuable.
records.prompt: Find three ways AI can save time in my week based on this schedule or task list: [paste schedule/task list]. Estimate the smallest first win.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Reclaim the first five minutes.
records.cta: Comment TIME if AI helped you reclaim even five minutes.

records.title: Understanding
records.lesson: Use AI with clear eyes. Know what it can help with, where it fails, and what still needs human review.
records.exercise: Make two lists: AI helps me with... and I still need to verify...
records.prompt: Explain what AI can and cannot do for this use case: [describe use case]. Give me strengths, limits, risks, and a smart beginner workflow.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Know the strengths and the limits.
records.download: U - Understanding Strengths and Limits Map
records.cta: Comment UNDERSTAND if the limits changed how you will use AI.

records.title: Vision
records.lesson: AI works best when it serves a clear vision. Without a vision, tools become noise.
records.exercise: Write one goal AI can support this month.
records.prompt: Help me connect AI to this bigger goal: [insert goal]. Break it into milestones, weekly actions, and one first prompt.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Vision leads the tech.
records.cta: Comment VISION with the goal AI will support.

records.title: Wisdom
records.lesson: Wisdom is knowing when to use AI, when to edit it, and when to leave the human moment untouched.
records.exercise: Take one AI answer and decide: use it, edit it, or reject it.
records.prompt: Help me decide whether to use, edit, or reject this AI output. Consider trust, timing, relationship, accuracy, and tone: [paste output].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Wisdom decides what belongs.
records.cta: Comment WISDOM with the choice you made.

records.title: eXecution
records.lesson: Execution is the bridge between inspiration and real change. Tiny shipped outputs compound.
records.exercise: Ship one tiny output today: a caption, email, plan, checklist, summary, or first draft.
records.prompt: Help me ship this tiny output in the next 20 minutes: [describe output]. Give me a draft, final checklist, and publish/send step.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Ship one tiny thing.
records.cta: Comment SHIPPED when one tiny output is done.

records.title: You
records.lesson: Your story, taste, and lived experience are still your edge. AI should support your voice, not erase it.
records.exercise: Ask AI to help express one personal story more clearly while keeping your voice.
records.prompt: Help me shape this personal story without making it generic. Preserve my voice, values, and details: [paste story].
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Your voice is the edge.
records.download: Y - You Voice and Story Worksheet
records.cta: Comment YOU if you used AI without losing yourself.

records.title: Zero Excuses
records.lesson: You can start with what you have right now. Five focused minutes is enough to begin.
records.exercise: Spend five minutes using one AI tool before today ends.
records.prompt: Give me a five-minute AI starter task based on what I have available right now: [describe phone/computer/time/goal]. Keep it simple enough to finish today.
records.video: 60-second teaching clip: Five minutes is enough to begin.
records.cta: Comment ZERO EXCUSES when you take the first step.
